name: Roasted Yellow Pepper Soup
recipeIngredient: 1 cup chicken stock or vegetable stock or water
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 400°F.
2. Place peppers on an oiled cookie sheet and place in the oven. Roast peppers, turning occasionally, for 20-30 minutes, until skins puff up and are mostly blackened.
3. While peppers are roasting, melt butter over low heat and cook onions gently with salt, pepper, and cumin for 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until translucent. Add garlic and cook until aroma is released. Sprinkle flour over and cook, stirring, another 5 minutes. Stir in stock or water and bring to a simmer.
4. Turn off heat and set a food mill fitted with a fine sieve over the pot.
5. Unwrap the bowl of peppers; they should be very soft. Pull out and discard cores. Place contents of the bowl, juice and all, into the food mill and pass through.
6. Remove the mill from the pot and clean out pepper skins and seeds by rotating the blade backwards.
7. Whisk the contents of the pot thoroughly and simmer for 10 minutes for the flavors to come together. Whisk in milk and pass through the food mill again into a clean pot, then heat gently and serve.
Yield: 4 to 6 servings
You could also make this soup with red peppers. Better yet, make both and serve a ladle of each in the same bowl.
When you buy peppers for roasting, choose those with flat sides as the very curvy ones don't roast evenly. Roasting over an open flame results in roasted peppers with a firmer texture, perfect for salads, etc. Oven roasting results in a softer pepper; using the food mill to puree eliminates the need to spend time peeling and seeding.
To make this meal even faster, you can roast and puree the peppers a day or two before. Roasted pepper puree also freezes beautifully.
